methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $157M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
BRINC Drones, a Series C defense and security drone company founded by Blake Resnick, has raised $157M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of ten same-sector comparables at similar or adjacent stages, spanning 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Saronic, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, using the $157M total raised as a baseline proxy, yielding $267M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the ten-company defense & security cohort, which shows a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. The upper-quartile performers (Saronic at $9.2B and Chaos Industries at $4.5B) represent later-stage outliers; mid-stage comparables like Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI cluster around $1.4B–$1.5B.

The triangulated analysis yields a low of $227M, median of $821M, and high of $1.6B. The wide interquartile spread reflects heterogeneity in stage, product maturity, and defense-sector positioning across the peer set. The stage-progression method produces a more conservative floor, while peer medians anchor the mid-point and ceiling. Key uncertainties include BRINC's relative market share within defense/security applications and whether its technology trajectory supports a premium or discount to the $1.4B peer median.
